architecture_fit.note: Genuine depth-matching QK attention on a sign embedding, MLP dropped, small delta from base_model; minor hand-coded routing for X/wrap cells dilutes the pure-attention claim.

baseline_comparison.note: Full circuit ~1.0 vs uniform baseline 0.37 and a depth-ablated control — clear strawman failing where the method succeeds.

faithfulness.note: Ablating the depth head collapses the circuit to uniform, a valid causal check; acknowledges the trained-model analog is activation-patching the depth feature.

operating_range.note: Holds at ~1.0 across every span length 3-8 (robustness 0.9999), though range is bounded by the task's 4-9 length strings.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-set: q=[2D(i),-1], phi(k)=[D(k),D(k)^2] as an explicit linear QK score, no learning.

visual_judgement.note: Depth-profile overlaid with the attention distribution (green = CYK-correct) makes the spike-at-balance-point claim directly legible, plus ablation and per-span panels.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties the shared split-position axes, the D(i) reference line, and the green correct bars to exactly what the goal asks.

notes: A strong hand-built attempt that nails the core insight — Dyck-1 CYK splits are depth-matching points D(k)=D(i) expressible as a genuine linear QK attention score — reaching ~1.0 canonical accuracy with +0.63 lift over uniform and near-perfect span robustness. It includes a real ablation control and clear, well-motivated visuals. The main caveat is hand-coded routing for X-cell and wrap-cell cases, which is heuristic rather than pure attention and slightly overstated as a fully attention-only circuit.
